name: Stewed Pork Intestine Over Wu-Ching Burner
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the pork intestine. Rub with salt to clean it, then rinse again.
2. Cut duck blood curd and sour cabbage into 1-inch cubes. Section the chilies and garlic cloves.
3. Cook the intestine in boiling water for a while, then cut into 1-inch long sections.
4. In a small pot, combine the pork intestine sections with the diced duck blood curd and sour cabbage.
5. Heat oil in a wok. Stir-fry ginger, garlic, and Chili Nam Yuey until fragrant.
6. Add chilies, peppercorns, salt, sugar, and soup stock to the wok. Bring to a boil.
7. Pour the sauce into the small pot with the pork intestine sections. Add the garlic cloves.
8. Simmer for 40 minutes over low heat.
9. Serve hot over the Wu-Ching burner. Drip some sesame oil on top before eating.
10. Serves 4 as a main dish and 6 as an appetizer. Can be served with a crusty French baguette and salad, or with broad noodles.
From The Chinese Regional Cuisine Series, Szechuan Cooking.
